g.text: Event Dispatch Loop — Reactor Main Iteration (reactor.c)
WHY steps 4/5/6 run AFTER step 3 — not interleaved
STEP 4 — Apply Pending epoll Modifications [safe: dispatching=0]
Step 3 (dispatch) | dispatching=1 | callbacks queue mutations — never call epoll_ctl directly
Step 4 (apply ops) | dispatching=0 | epoll_ctl DEL clears handler slots — fd reuse is now safe
Step 5 (timers) | dispatching=0 | timer callbacks see consistent handler table
Step 6 (deferred) | dispatching=0 | reactor_register() inside deferred CB → immediate epoll_ctl
Snapshot trick | drain_until | new reactor_defer() calls land in NEXT tick only
timeout = -1 | no timers pending — block forever
timeout = N | block up to N ms
events[i].events | uint32_t | EPOLLIN / EPOLLOUT / EPOLLERR / EPOLLHUP
events[i].data.fd | int | fd that is ready (or .ptr in M3)
Callback may call (safely deferred while dispatching=1)
reactor_defer() | enqueues to deferred ring buffer
reactor_set_timeout() | inserts into timer heap (safe — no epoll touch)
During Step 3, the events[] array is being iterated.
Calling epoll_ctl() during iteration can reuse fd numbers
if a DEL+close triggers OS fd recycling → stale pointer bug.
The dispatching=1 flag forces all mutations to be QUEUED,
then safely applied in Steps 4/5/6 after the loop completes.
return (int)(expiry - now); // ms until next timer
